acceptance_criteria.summary: Journal schema is versioned and records spec hash, route profile, workflow, slot, adapter, prompt/options hash, result, and metadata.
Journal save creates parent directories, writes through journal.json.tmp, and renames the temp file into place.

Corrupt journals are quarantined and incompatible schema versions raise JournalSchemaError with a reset message. Malformed entries (non-dict) now raise JournalSchemaError and quarantine the file instead of being silently coerced to an empty journal.
Workflow runtime binds journals to spec hash, route profile, and workflow, clears stale entries on identity change, and journals rich leaf entries.
Existing read-only workflow runtime spike tests remain green.

commits.message: test(CL-37yu): red - journal hardening tests for AC1-AC4
fix(CL-37yu): address codex adversarial findings — from_dict malformed entries

tests.result: 3 failed, 772 passed, 23 skipped; failures were missing op-credentials registration and external GitHub migration commit checks, not workflow journal/runtime behavior.
19 passed (includes new test_corrupt_journal_nondict_entries_raises_schema_error for the codex REGRESSION fix).

downstream_impacts: Workflow journals written by the spike runtime now use schema version 1 and reject incompatible on-disk versions.
Resume cache reuse is bound to workflow identity and no longer reuses entries across spec, route profile, or workflow changes.
